paper_title: A Model for Carbohydrate Metabolism in the Diatom Phaeodactylum tricornutum Deduced from Comparative Whole Genome Analysis

methods.description: This method involves screening and analyzing sequences from diatom genome sequencing projects to identify and model genes involved in carbohydrate metabolism.

steps.description: We screened sequences from the current JGI diatom genome sequencing projects for the diatoms Thalassiosira pseudonana v3.0 and Phaeodactylum tricornutum v2.0 using the BLAST algorithm.
steps.searchable_summary: screen diatom genome sequences using BLAST

steps.description: Comparison with the genome sequences of the red algae Cyanidioschyzon merolae and Galdieria sulphuraria as well as with other publicly available algal sequences helped to delimit gene modeling.
steps.searchable_summary: compare diatom sequences with red algae and other algal sequences

steps.description: Signal peptides of endoplasmic reticulum (ER) proteins were identified using the program SignalP.
steps.searchable_summary: identify ER protein signal peptides using SignalP

steps.description: The presence of a C-terminal retention signal (KDEL, DDEL or DEEL) was checked manually.
steps.searchable_summary: manually check for C-terminal retention signals

steps.description: Plastid proteins of diatoms possess bipartite targeting signals. We screened for signal peptides using SignalP and used ChloroP for prediction of chloroplast transit peptide-like domains.
steps.searchable_summary: screen for plastid protein signal peptides and predict transit peptide-like domains

steps.description: The NCBI Conserved Domain Search was used to identify N-terminal extensions from the conserved regions of the respective protein.
steps.searchable_summary: identify N-terminal extensions using NCBI Conserved Domain Search

steps.description: Mitochondrial transit peptides were identified using the program TargetP.
steps.searchable_summary: identify mitochondrial transit peptides using TargetP

steps.description: Putative enzymes without recognizable targeting sequences were considered cytosolic.
steps.searchable_summary: classify enzymes without targeting sequences as cytosolic
